manual_notes: This whole-game minute residual is better explained as an upstream source conflict than a remaining parser defect. The live output matches BBR minutes on 18 of 20 players, and the only two BBR misses are a single Vaughn/Hendrickson swap where tpdev_pbp stays much closer to the live output than to BBR. There are no event-on-court rows, no severe minute outliers, and the official box matches only 1 of 20 player-minute rows, so keep the raw residual visible but classify the game as source_limited_upstream_error rather than an open candidate_systematic_defect.

Damon Jones has no event-on-court rows in the live Block A rerun, and the current output (11.3) stays close to tpdev_pbp (11.68) while official and BBR both report 16.63. Treat this as a source-limited upstream minute conflict rather than a local lineup bug.

Kendall Gill has no event-on-court rows in the live Block A rerun, and the current output (36.7) stays close to tpdev_pbp (36.55) while official (32.72) and BBR (31.37) diverge by 3.98 to 5.33 minutes. Treat this as a source-limited upstream minute conflict rather than a local lineup bug.

This whole-game minute and PM residual is better explained as an upstream source conflict than a remaining parser defect. The Jeffries after-the-buzzer 0:00 three is already dropped by the live row override, counting-stat audit is clean, there are no event-on-court rows, and there are no severe minute outliers. After that drop, the live output still matches pbpstats minutes on 23 of 23 players and BBR minutes on 22 of 23 players, while the only BBR miss is the Steven/Steve Smith join issue and the official/tpdev box minutes match 0 of 23 player rows. Keep the raw residual visible, but classify the game as source_limited_upstream_error rather than an open lineup blocker.
